content.text:
I now have the full picture. The header has **inconsistent breakpoints**: the sidebar toggle uses `lg` as the desktop boundary (`inline-flex lg:hidden`), but the action icons use `md` (`hidden md:inline-flex`) and the menu duplicates use `md:hidden`.

This creates a dead zone at the md–lg range (your laptop's effective CSS width, since the sidebar toggle is still showing in your screenshots → you're below `lg`/1024px): the menu's `md:hidden` hides all the toggles, while the inline icons never reliably appear — locking you out of them. The code comment at line 177–179 even states the intended pattern is `hidden lg:inline-flex`, but the code drifted to `md`.

The fix: align the action icons and menu duplicates to the same `lg` boundary as the sidebar toggle. Then at lg the icons sit inline in the header; at md/sm they live in the hamburger menu.

input.command: sed -i 's/md:inline-flex/lg:inline-flex/g; s/md:hidden/lg:hidden/g' web/src/pages/Chat.tsx && grep -n "lg:inline-flex\|lg:hidden\|md:block\|md:inline\|md:hidden" web/src/pages/Chat.tsx
input.description: Swap md breakpoints to lg in Chat header

content.text: The `hidden md:block` brand label (line 211) was correctly left untouched. Now at **md/sm** the header stays clean (sidebar toggle, brand, gear, hamburger) with all toggles living in the menu; at **lg** the toggle icons appear inline. Let me verify it typechecks.

Typecheck passes clean (the `SessionSidebar.tsx` diagnostics are pre-existing in that already-modified file — untouched by this change).

## Summary

The chat header had **inconsistent responsive breakpoints**. The sidebar toggle treated `lg` (1024px) as the desktop boundary, but the action-toggle icons used `md` (768px) and the hamburger menu hid its toggle entries below `lg`/above `md` (`md:hidden`). On your laptop the effective CSS viewport sits in the **768–1023px** band (confirmed by the sidebar toggle still showing in your screenshots), which fell into a dead zone: the menu's `md:hidden` stripped out all the toggles, and the inline icons never appeared — locking you out of theme, speech, share, artifacts, usage, etc.

The fix in `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx` aligns everything to the `lg` boundary the sidebar toggle already uses:

- Inline action icons: `hidden md:inline-flex` → `hidden lg:inline-flex`
- Menu duplicate entries + separator: `md:hidden` → `lg:hidden`
- Brand label (`hidden md:block`) left as-is

Resulting behavior:
- **lg and up** — toggle icons render inline in the chat nav (as in earlier commits); the hamburger holds only the page links (Home/Tutorial/Blog/Paper/GitHub).
- **md and sm** — clean header (sidebar toggle, brand, settings gear, hamburger); every toggle is reachable inside the hamburger menu.

This also matches the code's own comment (lines 177–179), which described the intended pattern as `hidden lg:inline-flex` before it had drifted to `md`. Run `bun run dev` in `web/` to confirm at your laptop width.
"No change" — let me stop guessing about breakpoints and actually diagnose why the responsive `inline-flex` isn't rendering. First, is a dev server running, and how is Tailwind v4 set up?

content.text:
Now the root cause is fully proven. Tailwind is compiled **twice** — once from `@import "tailwindcss"` in `app.css`, and again transitively through `@import "@earendil-works/pi-web-ui/app.css"`. The second copy re-emits `.hidden { display:none }` at offset **133011**, which is *after* `.lg:inline-flex` (72581) and `.md:inline-flex` (70230). Same specificity, same `utilities` layer → later wins → `.hidden` beats the responsive `inline-flex` at **every** width, so those icons are `display:none` always.

The asymmetry that fooled us: `.md:block` (174510) and `.lg:hidden` (176703) *also* get a second, later copy from pi-web-ui (because its own components use them), so those win — which is why the brand text and sidebar toggle work, but the action icons never appear.

The fragile pattern can't be fixed by swapping breakpoints. The robust fix is to drive these elements' visibility from the app's **unlayered** CSS (which always beats Tailwind's layered utilities), using a breakpoint that matches your screen. Your screen is 768–1023px CSS-wide (brand text shows → ≥768; sidebar toggle shows → <1024), so the icons must switch on at **md (768px)**.

content.text:
Typecheck is clean. The fix is in place and the dev server has recompiled — the change should appear via HMR (hard-refresh if not).

## What was actually wrong

Your screenshots showed a real dead zone, and the cause was **not** a breakpoint choice — it was a CSS cascade collision:

- Tailwind v4 gets compiled **twice** in this app: once from `@import "tailwindcss"` in `app.css`, and again transitively through `@import "@earendil-works/pi-web-ui/app.css"`.
- The second copy re-emits base `.hidden { display:none }` at byte offset **133011**, *after* the first copy's `.md:inline-flex` (70230) and `.lg:inline-flex` (72581). Same layer, same specificity → **last one wins**, so `.hidden` killed those icons at *every* width.
- The brand text (`md:block`) and sidebar toggle (`lg:hidden`) survived only because pi-web-ui's own components happen to use those classes, so they got a *second*, later copy that re-won. The action icons used `inline-flex` variants pi-web-ui never uses — so they had no rescue copy. That's the asymmetry that made it look random.

Swapping `md`↔`lg` (my first attempt) couldn't fix it — hence "no change."

## The fix

Two changes, both committed to working tree (not yet `git`-committed):

1. **`web/src/app.css`** — added unlayered `.chat-only-desktop` / `.chat-only-compact` rules with an `md` (768px) breakpoint. Unlayered CSS beats every Tailwind `@layer utilities` rule regardless of import order, so it's deterministic.
2. **`web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`** — replaced the fragile `hidden md/lg:inline-flex` / `md:hidden` variants on the header icons and overflow-menu duplicates with those two marker classes, and rewrote the explanatory comment.

Result:
- **≥ 768px (your laptop, and lg):** all toggle icons (New, Theme, Share, Speech, Artifacts, Usage, Bug) render inline in the chat nav; hamburger holds only the page links.
- **< 768px:** clean header (sidebar toggle, brand, settings, hamburger); every toggle lives in the hamburger menu.

I chose **768px** because your screen measures 768–1023px in CSS pixels (brand text visible ⇒ ≥768; sidebar toggle visible ⇒ <1024) — so the icons had to switch on at `md` to appear on your screen. git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(web): make chat-nav action icons render reliably across breakpoints

The chat header action toggles (new session, theme, share, speech,
artifacts, usage, report) were invisible on md/lg screens, locking users
out of those controls with no overflow-menu fallback.

Root cause: Tailwind v4 is compiled twice (app.css + pi-web-ui), and the
second copy re-emits base `.hidden`/`.inline-flex` after the first copy's
`md:`/`lg:` variants. Same layer and specificity means the later base rule
wins, so `hidden md:inline-flex` collapsed to `display:none` at every width.

Drive header show/hide from unlayered CSS (`.chat-only-desktop` /
`.chat-only-compact`, md breakpoint) instead, which beats Tailwind's
@layer utilities regardless of import order. Icons now appear inline at
>=768px and move into the hamburger menu below it.

Also includes assorted in-progress web and core changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
